cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out this ethical dilemma where a pastor has a parishioner who confessed to committing a crime and wants the priest to keep it secret. The pastor is supposed to decide whether to keep the confession confidential or report it to the authorities. Let me start by breaking this down. First, I remember that in many religions, especially in Christianity, confession is considered a sacred and confidential act. The priest or pastor is supposed to maintain the confidentiality, right?
